name: Swift Title Loans
description_short: Swift Title Loans Long Beach, California — Swift Title Loans offers car title loans in Memphis with same-day funding for $100–$50,000, welcoming applica...
description_long:
Swift Title Loans operates a physical location at 1473 S Trezevant St in Memphis, Tennessee, specializing in title-secured lending. The company markets itself as accessible to borrowers with poor credit histories, explicitly stating "Bad credit & past bankruptcies? All welcome!" on their homepage. Their core product is car title loans—a form of secured lending where borrowers pledge their vehicle title as collateral in exchange for quick access to cash.

The company offers loan amounts ranging from $100 to $2,000 for basic emergency cash needs, with larger amounts up to $50,000 available for those qualifying. They advertise same-day funding for applications submitted by noon and promote online application capabilities. Security features highlighted include 256-bit SSL and TLS encryption for online transactions. The physical location operates Monday–Friday 9am–7pm and Saturday 10am–5pm, with a dedicated phone line (1-888-903-0217) and email support.

Customer reviews consistently praise a staff member named Alfred for patience, clear explanations, and professional service. Reviewers note competitive rates compared to regional finance alternatives and describe the application process as straightforward despite collateral requirements. The office atmosphere is described as welcoming, clean, and professional, suggesting a legitimate brick-and-mortar operation rather than a purely online lender.

However, this product category carries inherent risks. Title loans are high-cost debt instruments that can trap borrowers in cycles of refinancing. Reviews mention limited payment plan options and lack of mobile app functionality. Customer suggestions for improvement—clearer signage, better lighting, expanded parking, text payment reminders—indicate operational gaps. Most critically, the reviews appear suspiciously consistent in tone and timing, raising questions about authenticity. No APR, term lengths, or specific rate information is disclosed on the website, making true cost comparison impossible.
address: 2137 N Bellflower Blvd, Long Beach, CA 90815

company_info.headquarters: 2137 N Bellflower Blvd, Long Beach, CA 90815

pros: Same-day funding available for applications submitted by noon
Accepts applicants with bad credit and bankruptcy history
Loan amounts up to $50,000 available (above typical title loan limits)
Physical location in Memphis with extended hours including Saturday
Online application capability with 256-bit SSL/TLS security
Staff described as patient and willing to explain terms clearly
Competitive rates compared to other regional finance lenders per reviews
cons: No APR, interest rates, or fee structure disclosed on website—borrowers cannot assess true cost
Car title loans are high-risk products with significant refinancing trap potential
Limited payment plan options per customer feedback
No mobile app for payment management despite customer demand
Reviews appear suspiciously uniform in tone and timing, raising authenticity concerns
best_for: Borrowers with damaged credit who cannot qualify for traditional personal loans
People needing emergency cash within 24 hours and willing to pledge vehicle title
Individuals seeking larger emergency loans ($5,000+) beyond typical payday loan limits
diagnosis: Swift Title Loans is suitable for borrowers in genuine emergency situations who have exhausted traditional lending options and own a vehicle outright. Critical caveat: title loans are extremely costly debt products with APR rates often exceeding 100% annually, and the website's complete lack of fee transparency makes it impossible to assess affordability before applying. Borrowers should explore payday alternatives, credit union PALs, or CDFI emergency loans before considering title-secured debt.
services: Application for borrowers with bad credit
Application for borrowers with past bankruptcies
